texto: Who were these people, these specially selected tenants? They were mothers and fathers and children. A dressmaker, a secretary, an inventor, a doctor, a judge. And, oh yes, one was a bookie, one was a burglar, one was a bomber, and one was a mistake.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ellen Raskin que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “Who were these people, these specially selected tenants? frase_original: Who were these people, these specially selected tenants? They were mothers and fathers and children. A dressmaker, a secretary, an inventor, a doctor, a judge. And, oh yes, one was a bookie, one was a burglar, one was a bomber, and one was a mistake.

texto: Angela could not be the bomber, not that sweet, pretty thing. Thing? Is that how she regarded that young woman, as a thing? And what had she ever said to her except "I hear you're getting married, Angela" or "How pretty you look, Angela." Had anyone asked her about her ideas, her hopes, her plans? If I had been treated like that I'd have used dynamite, not fireworks; no, I would have just walked out and kept right on going. But Angela was different.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ellen Raskin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Angela could not be the bomber, not that sweet, pretty thing. Thing? Is that how she regarded tha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, sexism, angela pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e reflexao.

And what had she ever said to her except "I hear you're getting married, Angela" or "How pretty you look, Angela." Had anyone asked her about her ideas, her hopes, her plans? If I had been treated like that I'd have used dynamite, not fireworks; no, I would have just walked out and kept right on going. But Angela was different.

texto: Sandy fidgeted with his pen. “There’s something I didn’t write down. Maybe I shouldn’t tell you, you being a judge and all, but, well, Jake Wexler… he’s a bookie.”No, he should not have told her. “A small-time operator, I’m sure, Mr. McSouthers,” the judge replied coldly. “It can have no bearing on the matter before us. Sam Westing manipulated people, cheated workers, bribed officials, stole ideas, but Sam Westing never smoked or drank or placed a bet. Give me a bookie any day over such a fine, upstanding, clean-living man.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Ellen Raskin sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Sandy fidgeted with his pen. “There’s something I didn’t write down. Maybe I shouldn’t tell you, …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, morality, judge, útil em momentos de trabalho e reflexao.

texto: Life, too, is senseless unless you know who you are, what you want, and which way the wind blows.
explicacao: Citação de Ellen Raskin sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Life, too, is senseless unless you know who you are, what you want, and which way the wind blows.” articula reflexao, identity, senseless em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
